title: Tool System
subtitle: Tool System Design
description: Unified management, registration, discovery, and execution of tools with MCP protocol standardization. The tool system defines the boundary of Agent capabilities.
sub_problems: Tool registration: How to define and register tool schema (name, parameters, return values)
Tool discovery: How agents know which tools are available
Permission control: Which tools require user confirmation before execution
Result formatting: How to format tool return values into LLM-understandable text
MCP protocol: Model Context Protocol standardizes tool interfaces
Tool result summarization: How to compress raw tool output into LLM-consumable summaries
Tool call lifecycle: How to track all states from pending→running→success/failed
Graceful tool degradation: How to ensure core tools remain available when dependencies are missing
best_practices: Tool descriptions must be precise: LLMs select tools based on descriptions, vague descriptions cause misselection
Parameters should have defaults: Reduce the number of parameters LLMs need to decide
Return results should be structured: Facilitates LLM parsing and referencing
Dangerous operations need confirmation: File deletion, network requests require human-in-the-loop
Control tool quantity: Too many tools dilute LLM selection accuracy
Separate thin wrapper layer from service layer: tools/ handles interface adaptation only, logic goes in services/
Truncate tool results before summarization: Prevent oversized output from exhausting context window
Use AST inspection before code execution: ImportGuard intercepts dangerous modules before execution

values.DeepTutor: Module import + __init__.py unified re-export, no decorators/registry
YAML valid_tools whitelist + Agent-level tool subset assignment

values.DeerFlow(config): Built-in tools use @tool decorator, MCP tools merged via get_cached_mcp_tools()
values.DeerFlow(MCP): Full support for stdio/SSE/HTTP three transport types
values.MiroThinker: Native MCP, 7 types of StdioServerParameters tool servers
values.DeepTutor: Not supported, tool interface is project-internal Python functions

values.DeerFlow(config): Modify YAML use path to switch, requires restart
values.DeerFlow(MCP): mtime cache invalidation auto-detects config changes
values.MiroThinker: Loaded at startup, fixed during runtime
values.DeepTutor: Provider switchable at runtime (env/config), no tool hot reload
LLM-driven tool result summarization middleware, summarize after truncating to 2000 characters

solutions.title: config.yaml → ToolConfig → resolve_variable() reflection loading + tool_groups grouping + SubagentConfig whitelist/blacklist
solutions.description: Declarative tool configuration in config.yaml (name/group/use triplet), parsed by ToolConfig (Pydantic) then reflection-loaded via resolve_variable(): importlib.import_module(module_path) + getattr(module, variable_name) + isinstance type validation. Four tool_groups (web/file:read/file:write/bash), get_available_tools(groups=["web"]) filters by group. Community tools follow unified pattern: each tool under src/community/ decorated with @tool("tool_name"), reads YAML extra parameters via get_app_config().get_tool_config("tool_name").model_extra. SubagentConfig supports tools (whitelist) and disallowed_tools (blacklist, defaults to disallowing "task" to prevent recursive delegation). ConfigDict(extra="allow") enables parameter pass-through.

solutions.title: MCP protocol integration: McpServerConfig + get_cached_mcp_tools() mtime caching + three transport protocols
solutions.description: backend/src/mcp/ implements complete MCP integration in 4 files (~200 lines). McpServerConfig (Pydantic) defines enabled/type/command/args/env/url/headers. build_servers_config() iterates enabled servers to build langchain-mcp-adapters parameters with error isolation (single server failure doesn't affect others). get_mcp_tools() reads latest config from disk each time ensuring Gateway API changes take effect immediately. get_cached_mcp_tools() uses file mtime cache invalidation + asyncio.Lock prevents concurrency + ThreadPoolExecutor supports sync environments. Supports stdio/SSE/HTTP three transport protocols. resolve_env_variables() recursively resolves $ENV_VAR. Four-level config file lookup priority: parameters→environment variables→CWD→parent directory.

solutions.title: ToolManager MCP multi-server management + tool_blacklist + @with_timeout(1200) + fix_tool_call_arguments()
solutions.description: ToolManager (ToolManagerProtocol) in manager.py unifies management of multiple MCP servers. server_configs list defines 7 tool servers (tool-python/tool-google-search/tool-jina-scrape/tool-reasoning/tool-reader/tool-vqa/tool-transcribe), each configured with StdioServerParameters. get_all_tool_definitions() retrieves all tool schemas, execute_tool_call(server_name, tool_name, arguments) executes calls. tool_blacklist set disables specific tools. @with_timeout(1200) decorator adds 20-minute timeout protection to tool execution. fix_tool_call_arguments() corrects common LLM parameter errors. Supports persistent browser session storage.

solutions.title: Academic scenario tool system
solutions.description: In academic scenarios, tool results require LLM secondary summarization and citation tracking, representing an important extension dimension of the tool system
